title: Temperatures exceed 46°C in 20 Telangana districts on May 21
search_term: (heatwave OR "heat wave" OR "severe heatwave" OR "extreme heat" OR "heat spell" OR "scorching heat" OR "blistering heat" OR "sweltering heat" OR "hot winds" OR loo OR "hot loo winds") Telangana
full_text:
People in several parts of Telangana continued to experience high temperatures as 20 districts recorded maximum temperatures exceeding 46°C on Thursday (May 21, 2026) and the highest was 46.5°C. On Wednesday, over 46°C were recorded in 17 districts.
The highest of 46.5°C in two districts (Peddapalli and Suryapet) was followed by 46.4°C in 11 districts, 46.3°C in four districts and 46.2°C in two districts and 46.1°C in one district.
Temperatures in urban Telangana
The maximum temperature in Rangareddy was 44.9°C, 43.9°C in Medchal Malkajgiri and 43.4°C in Hyderabad.
Hot temperatures may persist in the State as India Meteorological Department-Hyderabad Centre on Friday has forecast heatwave conditions for Saturday (May 23, 2026) as very likely in isolated pockets of 18 districts — Adilabad, Kumaram Bheem Asifabad, Mancherial, Nirmal, Nizamabad, Jagtial, Rajanna Sircilla, Karimnagar, Peddapalli, Jayashankar Bhupalpally, Mulugu, Bhadradri Kothagudem, Khammam, Nalgonda, Suryapet, Mahabubabad, Warangal and Hanumakonda.
Besides, thunderstorms accompanied with lightning and gusty winds (40-50 kmph) were forecast as very likely to occur at isolated places in Vikarabad, Sangareddy, Medak, Kamareddy, Mahabubnagar, Nagarkurnool, Wanaparthy, Narayanpet and Jogulamba Gadwal.
Published - May 22, 2026 06:00 pm IST

title: Severe heatwave conditions forecast for multiple Telangana districts - The Hindu
full_text:
The India Meteorological Department forecast at 1 p.m. on Friday warned of severe heat wave conditions at isolated pockets in Karimnagar, Peddapalli, and Khammam today (May 22, 2026) with day temperatures likely above 45°C. The five-day bulletin cautions of severe heat wave conditions across multiple Telangana districts, with temperatures exceeding 45°C in several areas.
Heat wave conditions extend across a broad belt including Adilabad, Kumaram Bheem Asifabad, Mancherial, Nirmal, Nizamabad, Jagtial, Rajanna Sircilla, Jayashankar Bhupalpally, Mulugu, Bhadradri Kothagudem, Nalgonda, Suryapet, Mahabubabad, Warangal, Hanumakonda, Jangaon, and Siddipet. Warm night conditions are forecast in Bhadradri Kothagudem, Hanumakonda, Rangareddy, Hyderabad, and Medchal-Malkajgiri.
However, Hyderabad is expected to remain in the 41–44°C range throughout the five-day forecast period.
Districts like Suryapet, Karimnagar, Khammam, Jayashankar-Bhupalapally, Hanumakonda, and Bhadradri Kothagudem are projected to breach the 44°C mark on most days. The forecast temperature maps are orange and red as most of Telangana is covered under the ‘be prepared’ (41–44°C) or the extreme ‘warning’ (≥45°C) categories for day 1 and 2, with modest improvement from day 3 onward.
Thunderstorms with lightning and gusty winds of 40–50 kmph are forecast for May 22 and May 23 in Vikarabad, Sangareddy, Medak, Kamareddy, Mahabubnagar, Nagarkurnool, Wanaparthy, Narayanpet, and Jogulamba Gadwal districts.
Dry weather is expected to prevail across the State from May 24, with heat wave warnings gradually tapering off. Residents in the affected districts were advised by the IMD to limit outdoor exposure during peak afternoon hours and stay hydrated.
Published - May 22, 2026 03:10 pm IST

title: Red Alert for Telangana: Severe heat wave warning issued as temperatures set to soar - NewsMeter
search_term: ("heat advisory" OR "heat action plan" OR "heat alert" OR "IMD warning" OR "IMD alert" OR "red alert" OR "orange alert" OR "yellow alert" OR "NDMA advisory" OR "cooling shelter" OR "relief measures" OR "heat compensation" OR "severe weather warning") Telangana
full_text:
Red Alert for Telangana: Severe heat wave warning issued as temperatures set to soar
Orange Alert for heatwave conditions has been issued by the IMD, asking people to stay indoors
By Newsmeter NetworkHyderabad: ‘Severe Heat Wave’ when the maximum temperature departure from normal is 6.6°C or higher
Hyderabad: The India Meteorological Department (IMD) has issued a Red Alert for several districts in Telangana as a severe heat wave alert was issued for Friday, May 22.
According to the Meteorological Centre in Hyderabad, intense heat conditions are expected to peak over the next 24 hours, with a gradual rise in maximum temperatures by 1 to 2°C across the state.
Govt issues safety instructions across state
On Day 1, May 22, ‘Severe Heat Wave’ conditions are very likely to prevail in isolated pockets of Karimnagar, Peddapalli and Khammam. These areas are under a Red Alert, necessitating immediate action and adherence to official safety instructions.
Simultaneously, an Orange Alert for ‘Heat Wave’ conditions has been issued for a vast number of districts, including Adilabad, Komuram Bheem Asifabad, Mancherial, Nirmal, Nizamabad, Jagtial, Rajanna Sircilla, Jayashankar Bhupalpally, Mulugu, Bhadradri Kothagudem, Nalgonda, Suryapet, Mahabubabad, Warangal, Hanumakonda, Jangaon, and Siddipet.
The heat is not expected to subside quickly
The IMD has extended Heat Wave warnings (Yellow Alert) through May 26, covering various districts including Mancherial, Jayashankar Bhupalpally, and Suryapet. Additionally, ‘Warm Night’ conditions are forecast for Hyderabad, Medchal-Malkajgiri, Rangareddy and Bhadradri Kothagudem, where high minimum temperatures will offer little respite after sunset.
How is a heatwave classified?
The IMD classifies a ‘Severe Heat Wave’ when the maximum temperature departure from normal is 6.6°C or higher, or when the actual temperature touches or exceeds 47°C. Potential impacts include a high risk of heat exhaustion and heatstroke.
Public advisory on heat
Citizens are strongly advised to take precautions: drink plenty of water, wear light-coloured cotton clothing, and avoid direct exposure to the sun between 12:00 pm and 4:00 pm.

title: Heatwave in Telangana with temperatures above 40°C - The News Mill
full_text:
Telangana is experiencing intense heatwave conditions with maximum temperatures exceeding 40°C in several areas over the past two days, according to the India Meteorological Department (IMD) Scientist Dharmaraju. He stated that dry weather conditions are prevailing across the state and temperatures are expected to increase further in the coming days.
“For the past two days, Telangana State has been experiencing maximum temperatures above 40°C. Dry weather conditions are currently prevailing over the state,” Dharmaraju said.
He noted that several districts have recorded high temperatures, including Adilabad at around 41°C and Nizamabad at 41.4°C. Cities such as Medak, Hanumakonda, Khammam, and Mahabubnagar also reported temperatures near or above 40°C. “The city recorded a maximum temperature of 40.1°C yesterday,” he added, warning that temperatures may rise by 2°C to 3°C over the next five days.
The IMD morning bulletin predicted changing weather patterns across various parts of the country. A hot and humid weather warning was issued for multiple regions, including isolated pockets of Telangana, Rayalaseema, Madhya Maharashtra, North Interior Karnataka, Marathwada, Madhya Pradesh, Vidarbha, Chhattisgarh, Odisha, West Rajasthan, and Jharkhand between April 15 and 20, with differing peak periods.
The IMD also forecast likely hot and humid conditions in isolated areas of Gangetic West Bengal and Odisha during April 15-20, and in coastal Maharashtra, coastal Gujarat, Tamil Nadu, Kerala, coastal Andhra Pradesh, and coastal Karnataka during intervals between April 15 and 18. Authorities advised the public to remain cautious amid rising temperatures and uncomfortable weather.
In Northeast India, light to moderate rainfall accompanied by thunderstorms, lightning, and gusty winds is expected over Assam, Meghalaya, Arunachal Pradesh, Nagaland, Manipur, Mizoram, and Tripura between April 15 and 18, with heavy rainfall in some locations during this time.
Northwest India is forecast to receive rainfall and snowfall in Jammu and Kashmir, Ladakh, Himachal Pradesh, Punjab, and Haryana, along with thunderstorms, strong winds, and hailstorms in certain areas of Jammu and Kashmir.
East India anticipates scattered rainfall with thunderstorms in West Bengal, Sikkim, and Odisha at various times from April 15 to 20. In South India, light to moderate rain with thunderstorms and gusty winds is expected over Interior and Coastal Karnataka between April 15 and 18.
The IMD advised people to remain alert as weather conditions may vary across regions.
